===Pokémon=== Lusamine's Pokémon are kept in [[Ultra Ball]]s. ====Pokémon Sun and Moon==== =====First battle===== Winning this battle is not mandatory. {{Party/end}} =====Second battle===== Each of Lusamine's Pokémon has an aura that boosts one of its [[stat]]s, similar to the aura seen on the [[Totem Pokémon]]. Her Pokémon all have a perpetual angry expression, the same as when a Pokémon uses a [[Z-Move]]. Despite this, no Z-Moves are used within the fight and all her Pokémon lack any [[Z-Crystal]]s. The player will not gain [[prize money]] after winning. {{Party | background = {{locationcolor/light|space}} | border = {{locationcolor/dark|space}} | headcolor = {{white color}} | sprite = VSLusamine 2.png | size = 150px | prize = none | class = Aether President | classlink = Lusamine | name = Lusamine | location = Ultra Deep Sea | locationname = Ultra Space | game = SM | pokemon = 5 }} {{Pokémon | gen = 7 | ndex = 0036 | pokemon = Clefable | gender = female | level = 50

Their merged appeared to have empowered her Pokémon and made them angrier during the battle. After the combined Lusamine is defeated in battle, [[Nebby]] forcefully separates the two, and Nihilego returns to its Beast Ball, which falls next to Lusamine. It's unknown if Nihilego was left behind in Ultra Space as the player and the others escaped. None of Nihilego's moves are known.}} ====Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon==== Lusamine uses this team both in the one-time battle at Aether Paradise and in the [[Pokémon League (Alola)/Title Defense|Title Defense]] battles at the {{al|Pokémon League}}. Winning against Lusamine during the main story is not mandatory. The player will not [[black out]] and the story will progress either way. {{Party | background = {{white color}} | border = {{day color dark}} | headcolor = {{morning color light}} | sprite = Silvally |type1=normal |img=Gladion with Silvally.png |epnum=Generation VII |epname=Pokémon Sun, Moon,
Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon |desc=This {{p|Silvally}}, along with two others, was a research subject of the [[Aether Foundation]], genetically created as a part of the Beast Killer Project (BKP) under the name Type: Full. After being deemed a failure, the Pokémon was put into a cryogenic state, was given a helmet to restrict its powers and then renamed to {{p|Type: Null}}. Sometime prior to the events of the game, [[Gladion]] had stolen a Type: Null, managing to evolve it by the end of the game.}} =====Stolen by [[Lillie]]===== {{TrainerPoké |main=yes |trainer=Lillie |nick=Nebby |type1=psychic |img=Nebby SM.png |img2=Nebby Cosmoem SM.png |cap1=Nebby as a Cosmog alongside Lillie |cap2=Nebby as a Cosmoem |epnum=Generation VII |epname=Pokémon Sun, Moon,
Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon |desc=This {{p|Cosmog}}, nicknamed '''Nebby''' (Japanese: '''ほしぐもちゃん''' ''Hoshigumo-chan'') by Lillie, was a research subject of the [[Aether Foundation]]. Lillie rescued the Pokémon when she learned that the organization's experiments would eventually kill it, and thanks to its ability to teleport, was able to flee. Since escaping, Lillie has been taking Nebby to the ruins of the [[guardian deities]] of [[Alola]], believing she can learn something about Nebby's true nature. Eventually being stolen back by Lusamine in a set up with [[Team Skull]], Lusamine uses its power to open Ultra Wormholes around Alola. Nebby evolves into {{p|Cosmoem}} during the events of it and Lillie's kidnapping by the Aether Foundation. It then evolves into {{p|Solgaleo}}{{sup/7|SUS}}/{{p|Lunala}}{{sup/7|MUM}}, and after confronting Lusamine{{sup/7|SM}}/{{p|Necrozma}}{{sup/7|USUM}}, is left in the custody of the player.}}
